text: The surgeon uses the left robotic arm to slide a ring off a vertical peg using the instrument tips, then transfers it to the right arm. The ring is then returned to a horizontal peg, with the surgeon twisting their fingers to align the ring with the peg.
The surgeon uses the left robotic arm to twist the fingers, aligning and placing a ring onto a peg positioned at one of three different levels, demonstrating efficient camera navigation.

The surgeon uses the tips of the robotic instruments to gently grasp a ring, transferring it from one arm to the other while releasing it with the left arm to prevent the ring from getting caught on the instrument tip.
The surgeon demonstrates coordinated movement of both robotic arms using a foot pedal, ensuring the instruments remain within the camera's view.

The surgeon keeps their forearms on the armrest for stability while using the ender wrist. They push up to get an overview of the flashing ring positioned on the third level, reducing the distance needed to reach it.
The left robotic arm turns red, indicating excessive force as the ring is pulled off the peg forcefully.

The surgeon demonstrates sliding the ring off the peg instead of pulling it, highlighting a more efficient technique. The video shows an example of poor economy of motion, with the instrument moving in multiple directions before reaching the target, contrasting with a more direct approach.
